findings.claim: native command graph not yet implemented
findings.expected: universal-launcher should contain real registry, schema routing, dry-run, audit, and handlers
findings.observed: split repo is scaffolded; native FacMan CLI currently owns the smoke behavior
findings.recommendation: Build command registry in universal-launcher before more GUI work.

findings.expected: Python should be tooling, fixtures, validators, and tests only
findings.observed: apps/python_cli and product pyproject.toml are removed; native facman is built by CMake

findings.claim: universal setup and launcher are sibling repositories
findings.expected: FacMan must not own universal setup or launcher runtime implementation
findings.observed: Factorio repo keeps only include/flb and runtime/factorio; universal code moved out
findings.recommendation: Harden universal repo validators before large native code expansion.

findings.expected: no src/, source/, launcher/, product/, universal/, data/, schemas/, or packaging roots
findings.observed: structure policy and filesystem match this rule

findings.claim: apps/ contains frontend entrypoints and presentation
findings.expected: reusable behavior should live under runtime/ or universal repos
findings.observed: app roots use role names and do not contain nested src/source directories

findings.claim: Factorio repo must not implement install mutation
findings.expected: repair, uninstall, rollback, and destructive setup mutation belong to Universal Setup
findings.observed: docs and policies state this boundary
findings.recommendation: Keep setup mutation behind handoff/adapter boundaries.

findings.expected: launch plans should be previewed unless execution is explicit
findings.observed: README, product policy, and AIDE safety policy encode dry-run

findings.claim: no Factorio binaries may be bundled
findings.expected: package manifests and product policy must forbid bundling Factorio binaries

findings.claim: production legacy packages must not depend on Python or AIDE
findings.expected: AIDE and Python are development tooling surfaces only
findings.observed: package check and structure policy enforce runtime separation
findings.recommendation: Keep AIDE and Python out of package manifests.
